Label: Dexamethasone
Definition: Systemic glucocorticoid therapy (dexamethasone 6 mg once daily for up to 10 days) for patients hospitalized with COVID-19.

Label: Mechanical ventilation
Definition: Mechanical ventilation strategy designed to minimize alveolar injury from cyclic collapse/reopening and overdistention, involving higher PEEP, lower tidal volumes, and pressure-limited modes.

Label: Fluid management
Definition: Conservative fluid management strategy applied for 7 days in patients with acute lung injury.

Label: Delirium
Definition: Duration of delirium during critical illness, associated with long-term cognitive and executive function impairment.

Label: Positive end-expiratory pressure
Definition: Mechanical ventilation with higher positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) levels in patients with acute lung injury and ARDS.

Label: Lung ultrasound
Definition: Systematic lung ultrasound assessment including artifacts, lung sliding, consolidation, and pleural effusion to differentiate causes of acute respiratory failure.
Synonym: lung ultrasound for acute respiratory failure diagnosis

Label: Low tidal volume ventilation
Definition: Ventilation strategy to minimize alveolar recruitment/derecruitment and overdistention, thereby reducing ventilation-induced pulmonary and systemic cytokine response.

Label: spontaneous breathing trial
Definition: Ventilator weaning using a single daily trial of spontaneous breathing, rather than multiple or continuous trials.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Latent class analysis to identify distinct subphenotypes within acute respiratory distress syndrome based on clinical and biological data.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: 3 categories of ARDS based on hypoxemia severity (mild, moderate, severe), with explicit criteria for timing, edema origin, and chest radiograph.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Ventilation strategy using low tidal volumes, recruitment maneuvers, and high positive end-expiratory pressure for acute lung injury and acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Label: Early mobilization
Definition: A protocol using an ICU mobility team to initiate physical therapy within 48 hours of mechanical ventilation for acute respiratory failure.

Label: Dexamethasone
Definition: Intravenous dexamethasone 20 mg daily for 5 days, then 10 mg daily for 5 days or until ICU discharge, for treatment of COVID-19-associated ARDS.

Label: Prone positioning
Definition: Placing patients in a prone (face down) position for six or more hours daily for 10 days.

Label: acute chest syndrome
Definition: Treatment protocol including matched transfusions, bronchodilators, and bronchoscopy for acute chest syndrome in sickle cell disease.
Synonym: Treatment protocol for acute chest syndrome in sickle cell disease

Label: Non-invasive ventilation
Definition: Non-invasive ventilation to improve survival and quality of life in patients with amyotrophic lateral sclerosis.

Label: Mechanical ventilation
Definition: Mechanical ventilation with PEEP adjusted according to esophageal pressure measurements to estimate transpulmonary pressure and optimize alveolar recruitment.
Synonym: Mechanical ventilation guided by esophageal pressure

Label: restrictive red blood cell transfusion
Definition: Maintain hematocrit ≥24% in patients undergoing cardiac surgery with cardiopulmonary bypass.
Synonym: Restrictive red blood cell transfusion strategy

Label: Epidural anesthesia and analgesia
Definition: Use of epidural anaesthesia intraoperatively and epidural analgesia postoperatively for major abdominal surgery.

Label: Positive-Pressure Ventilation
Definition: Noninvasive ventilation delivered through a face mask for patients with acute respiratory failure.

Label: Growth Hormone Administration
Definition: Administration of growth hormone to critically ill adults hospitalized for prolonged periods.

Label: methylprednisolone therapy
Definition: Prolonged administration of methylprednisolone (initial dose 2 mg/kg/day, duration 32 days) for unresolving ARDS.

Label: Pulmonary Artery Catheter
Definition: Use of pulmonary artery catheter to guide hemodynamic management in patients with acute lung injury.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome

Label: Pulmonary Artery Catheter
Definition: Pulmonary artery catheter for hemodynamic monitoring in critically ill patients.

Label: Non-invasive Ventilation

Label: Positive end-expiratory pressure

Label: Prone positioning

Label: Pneumonia

Label: inflammasome pathway regulation
Definition: The inflammasome pathway and its downstream cytokines play critical roles in ARDS development.

Label: Lactate measurement

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Rosuvastatin, a 3-hydroxy-3-methylglutaryl coenzyme A reductase inhibitor, administered to critically ill patients with sepsis-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Definition: Persistent inflammatory cytokine signaling (IL-1beta, IL-8, ENA-78) is a proposed mechanism driving the persistent inflammatory process and severity of ARDS.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Oral simvastatin 80mg daily for up to 28 days in patients with acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Definition: Procalcitonin serum level measurement for discrimination of septic vs nonseptic causes of early acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Definition: Machine learning models using clinical variables to assign ARDS patients to hyperinflammatory or hypoinflammatory subphenotypes.

Definition: Corticosteroid use 7 days prior to ICU admission for critically ill H1N1 patients.

Label: Treatment effect heterogeneity
Definition: Awareness that patients in critical care RCTs have wide variability in risk and thus in the absolute benefit they can derive from a given therapy.
Synonym: Heterogeneity of treatment effect awareness in critical care RCTs

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Advanced age 70 years or older in patients with acute lung injury and ARDS.
Synonym: old age >=70 in acute lung injury and ARDS

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Ketoconazole, a synthetic imidazole with anti-inflammatory activity, given enterally at 400 mg/day for up to 21 days for early treatment of acute lung injury (ALI) and acute respiratory distress syndrome (ARDS).
Synonym: ketoconazole for early treatment of ALI and ARDS

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Prospective observational study to identify prevalence of hyperinflammatory and hypoinflammatory ARDS phenotypes in critically ill patients with COVID-19-related ARDS.
Synonym: Identification of ARDS phenotypes in COVID-19

Label: Inflammatory response
Definition: Upregulation of proinflammatory cytokines IL-1, IL-6, and TNF-alpha in bronchoalveolar lavage of patients with status asthmaticus, despite concomitant increase in antiinflammatory mediators.

Label: Fluid responsiveness assessment
Definition: Resuscitation of septic shock guided by assessment of fluid responsiveness using passive leg raise to determine fluid bolus or vasopressor administration.
Synonym: Resuscitation guided by fluid responsiveness assessment

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Application of noninvasive positive pressure ventilation in the emergency department for patients with acute respiratory distress.
Synonym: noninvasive positive pressure ventilation in acute respiratory distress

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Inhalation of low concentrations of nitric oxide to improve oxygenation and reduce pulmonary vascular resistance in severe ARDS.
Synonym: Inhaled nitric oxide for severe ARDS

Label: Respiratory Failure, Postoperative
Definition: Predictive model using preoperative factors to estimate risk of postoperative respiratory failure requiring prolonged mechanical ventilation or unplanned intubation.

Label: Continuous positive airway pressure
Definition: Prophylactic nasal continuous positive airway pressure at 10 cmH2O for at least 6 hours following cardiac surgery to prevent postoperative pulmonary complications.
Synonym: Prophylactic nasal continuous positive airway pressure (nCPAP) following cardiac surgery

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Intravenous administration of the xanthine derivative lisofylline (3 mg/kg q6h, max 300 mg) for early treatment of acute lung injury and acute respiratory distress syndrome.
Synonym: Lisofylline for early treatment of acute lung injury / ARDS

Label: Endotracheal intubation
Definition: Intubation within 24 hours of ICU admission for critically ill COVID-19 patients

Label: PEEP titration
Definition: Individualized ventilation strategy combining PEEP titration to best respiratory system compliance and assessment of alveolar recruitment in patients with COVID-19-induced ARDS.
Synonym: Individualised PEEP titration and alveolar recruitment in COVID-19 ARDS

Label: Sarilumab
Definition: Monoclonal antibody interleukin-6 receptor inhibitor

Label: High-Frequency Oscillatory Ventilation
Definition: High frequency oscillatory ventilation for acute lung injury and ARDS.

Label: Open lung ventilation strategy
Definition: Individualised ventilation strategy during one-lung ventilation combining alveolar recruitment manoeuvre to 40 cmH2O with PEEP titrated to best respiratory system compliance plus postop HFNO.
Synonym: individualised open-lung approach during one-lung ventilation

Label: High-flow nasal cannula oxygen therapy
Definition: Preoxygenation using high-flow nasal cannula oxygen combined with non-invasive ventilation, for hypoxaemic patients requiring intubation in the ICU.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Proinflammatory cytokine-primed neutrophils exhibit inappropriate adhesion and transendothelial migration, contributing to ARDS-associated lung injury.

Label: Positive end-expiratory pressure
Definition: Decreasing PEEP in 5 cmH2O steps from 20/15 to 0 cmH2O in patients with acute lung injury to determine alveolar derecruitment and optimal PEEP.
Synonym: decremental PEEP in acute lung injury

Label: Respiratory Physiotherapy
Definition: Respiratory physiotherapy techniques including deep breathing, directed cough, and postural drainage to prevent pulmonary complications after abdominal surgery.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Assessment of pulmonary function abnormalities and their relationship to health-related quality of life in survivors of acute respiratory distress syndrome.
Synonym: pulmonary function and health-related quality of life in ARDS survivors

Label: Tidal volume ventilation
Definition: Ventilation with high tidal volumes, identified as an independent risk factor for developing acute lung injury in patients with severe brain injury.

Label: hypercapnia
Definition: Supplemental carbon dioxide administration to critically ill patients to raise CO2 concentrations and promote the protective effects of hypercapnia while reducing the injurious effects of hypocapnia.

Label: Airway Pressure Release Ventilation
Definition: Airway pressure release ventilation mode, with lower airway pressures and improved oxygenation parameters compared to volume-controlled inverse ratio ventilation in patients with acute lung injury.

Label: Critically ill patients
Definition: Cooling to reduce oxygen consumption and cardiac output in febrile critically ill patients, potentially facilitating resuscitation and preventing hypoxic injury.
Synonym: Cooling for febrile critically ill patients

Label: Factor VIIa, Tissue
Definition: Recombinant factor VIIa, a hemostatic agent approved for hemophilia, increasingly used for off-label indications.

Label: Acute lung injury
Definition: Acute lung injury caused by trauma, with less severe lung epithelial and endothelial injury compared to other causes of acute lung injury.

Label: Acute lung injury
Definition: Depressive symptoms and impaired physical function are common and long-lasting sequelae in acute lung injury survivors.
Synonym: depression and physical impairment after acute lung injury

Label: time-cycled inspiratory pressure support ventilation
Definition: Time-cycled expiratory trigger during noninvasive inspiratory pressure support ventilation to improve patient-ventilator synchronization in the presence of mask air leaks.

Label: mechanical ventilation trigger mode
Synonym: Time Cycled Vs Flow Cycled Expiratory Trigger

Label: Oxygenation
Definition: Targeting partial pressure of arterial oxygen (Pao2) of 9 to 10 kPa (68 to 75 mmHg) during mechanical ventilation of comatose cardiac arrest survivors.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Increased N-terminal procollagen III peptide levels in bronchoalveolar lavage fluid associated with increased risk of mortality in patients with adult respiratory distress syndrome.
Synonym: elevated procollagen III levels in ARDS BALF

Label: Sevoflurane
Definition: Inhaled sevoflurane for sedation in acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Label: Critically ill patients
Definition: Condition characterized by impaired gas exchange (PaO2/FiO2 <200 mmHg) requiring respiratory support in critically ill ICU patients.
Synonym: acute respiratory failure in critically ill patients

Label: PEEP titration
Definition: Individualised PEEP titration guided by respiratory system compliance to optimise alveolar recruitment in ARDS.
Synonym: PEEP titration guided by respiratory system compliance in ARDS

Label: Neuromuscular blockade
Definition: 48-hour infusion of cisatracurium besylate neuromuscular blockade for treatment of ARDS.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Use of extracorporeal lung assist (ECLA) in the management of severe ARDS.

Label: Plateau pressure
Definition: Maintaining end-inspiratory plateau pressure (Pplat) below 35 cmH2O to prevent barotrauma in ARDS patients.
Synonym: Limiting plateau pressure during mechanical ventilation

Label: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation
Definition: Extracorporeal membrane oxygenation to support gas exchange in patients with acute lung injury due to H1N1 influenza.

Label: Positive end-expiratory pressure
Definition: Application of positive end-expiratory pressure (PEEP) in patients with acute lung injury to recruit collapsed alveoli and improve respiratory system mechanics.

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Intravenous hydrocortisone 50mg every 6 hours in adults with sepsis-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Label: COVID-19
Definition: ICU management including invasive mechanical ventilation, prone positioning, and ECMO for patients with severe COVID-19 requiring ICU admission.

Label: neurally adjusted ventilatory assist
Definition: Ventilation mode where assistance is provided in proportion to diaphragm electrical activity.

Label: Non-invasive ventilation

Label: Lung recruitment maneuver
Definition: Performing alveolar recruitment maneuvers (40 cmH2O pressure-supported breaths) during endotracheal suctioning to prevent suctioning-induced alveolar derecruitment in acute lung injury.

Label: Mechanical ventilation
Definition: Multivariate model identifying 6 early predictors (time to admission <7d, inability to cough/stand/lift elbows or head, liver enzyme increases) that identify Guillain-Barré patients at high risk of requiring mechanical ventilation.
Synonym: early predictors of mechanical ventilation in Guillain-Barré syndrome

Label: Body Mass Index
Definition: Body mass index as a prognostic factor in acute respiratory distress syndrome.

Label: Pulmonary Vascular Diseases

Label: Prone positioning

Label: Acute respiratory distress syndrome
Definition: Administration of aspirin, 325-mg loading dose followed by 81 mg/day, initiated within 24 hours of ED presentation and continued to hospital day 7, discharge, or death.

Label: E-PRE-DELIRIC model
Definition: Prediction model for delirium risk at ICU admission based on 9 factors: age, history of cognitive impairment, alcohol abuse, blood urea nitrogen, admission category, urgent admission, mean arterial pressure, corticosteroid use, and respiratory failure.

Label: Protein C, Activated

Label: Acute lung injury

Label: unplanned extubation prevention

Label: Granulocyte-Macrophage Colony-Stimulating Factor
